entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 28 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-28 07:34AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: Monkeypox declared ‘nationally significant’ by CHO; inflation hits 6.1 per cent; negotiations over climate bill continue
  • Norman plans Australian tournament, promises LIV won’t clash with majors
  • ‘Eating a banana with a knife and fork’: WA ‘teal’ independent Kate Chaney takes on parliament
  • Liam Jones stops playing QAFL as he eyes AFL return
  • Dead Saudi sisters were ‘like two little sparrows ... scared of something’
  • Competition heats up for OC places as more than 15,000 sit test
  • Race-by-race prview and tips for Goulburn on Friday
  • Anthony Koletti sings on: AVO dropped against Melissa Caddick’s husband
  • Emails reveal Ayres added candidate to trade commissioner role shortlist
  • Sydney apartment where Saudi sisters were found dead
  • Aunt of Aboriginal man who died in river disgusted by police comment, inquest told
  • Trial ordered on Chinese influence charge
  • Frankie Davidson: rock’n’roll pioneer ‘a true force field’
  • Best of cartoons, July 28, 2022
  • Inflation on track for 32-year high as wages go backwards: Chalmers
  • New Liberal MP thanks ousted party member for ‘courage’ in first speech
  • The Greens’ next move could define our politics for decades
  • Climate bill to make government fossil fuel investment harder
  • Aged care providers can seek exemption to Labor’s 24/7 nurse mandate
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • Cartoons of the week
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, July 27, 2022
  • Real wages slump, unemployment to rise as inflation’s headwinds buffet economy
  • OECD head Mathias Cormann dodges questions about WA Liberals ‘mess left behind’
  • Indigenous Voice could drive a wedge between Australians: Price
  • Where inflation is hurting household budgets the most
  • Construction watchdog vows to keep prosecuting unions after ‘negligible’ changes
  • The slugger, the jabber and the ref: the new order in question time
  • Witness tells court he saw NRL player plunge knife into youth leader’s back
  • Leeches, bloodletting, enemas and emetics: The uncomfortable history of medicine
  • Police seek two further arrests in Germany over Simone Strobel murder
  • Mother of nine-year-old who was shot dead charged with gun theft
  • Meet the 13-year-old maths whiz who’s this year’s youngest HSC student
  • NSW to ‘chase and recruit’ public school teachers as private sector snaps up graduates
  • Council’s trucks have mixed recycling with rubbish for ‘more than a decade’
  • Mystery surrounds lives and deaths of Saudi sisters in ‘unfriendly’ units
  • Rainbow jersey refusal is homophobia, plain and simple
  • Can you answer these 50 HSC questions?
  • HSC students display resilience and strength
  • How to set yourself up for HSC success
  • How to cope with the stresses of the HSC
  • Winning the numbers game in HSC maths
  • The science student who took on the world - and won
  • Bali records ‘zero’ foot and mouth but experts say disease lurks
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • From rubbish tip to urban forest: Pakistanis plant a new oasis
  • A family comes home, even if only tears are left after air strikes
  • Sect leader, self-help author and political player: The mystery man behind Morrison’s Tokyo speech
  • Largest pink diamond in 300 years discovered in Angola
  • US, Russia make overtures, reassurances about detainees, space cooperation
  • Dozens killed and injured in ‘social media-fuelled’ anti-UN protests
  • Surprise breakthrough for Biden, Democrats on healthcare, energy and climate spending
  • ‘Pure Nazi’: Hungarian leader’s ‘mixed race’ speech triggers high-profile resignation
  • As East-West tensions rise, Russia cuts gas flows further to Europe
  • Magnitude-7.1 earthquake hits Philippines’ most populous island
  • WHO chief advises reducing sex partners to avoid monkeypox
  • Wuhan locks down 1 million residents in echo of pandemic start
  • ‘Extremely traumatic’: Elderly woman threatened with barbecue fork in St Kilda
  • Nine newsreader Tracy Vo tops off Kimberley holiday with engagement announcement
  • Buying before they beat it: Crowds queue for cake shop set to close
  • CBA boss calls for local training boost amid skill shortfall
  • Philip Lowe and Jim Chalmers should become fluent in ‘Macquarie Speak’
  • Graphite miner Syrah lands loan with Biden administration
  • ABC’s Sydney-based radio, news teams to relocate to Parramatta by 2024
  • Perfect storm: This could be the recession we have to have
  • ‘Never in my wildest dreams’: F45 unveils disastrous update, CEO exits
  • The Wrap: ASX upbeat despite economic slowdown forecast
  • Fortescue breaks iron ore record for third year in a row
  • Macquarie’s Glenn Stevens says ‘be alert’ to risk of global recession
  • The face of a crisis: Asia’s richest woman loses half her fortune
  • Facebook’s parent Meta posts first revenue decline in history
  • Powell says US not in recession as Fed unleashes another big rate hike
  • Westpac, NAB leave door open on oil and gas finance amid climate push
  • Port backflip a boost for Viva’s Victorian gas terminal
  • Rio Tinto halves dividend as falling iron ore price hits profit
  • ‘I was the only openly gay kid at school’: filmmaker Goran Stolevski
  • This genre-bending comedy thriller is not what it seems
  • How did a glassblowing show become my obsession?
  • I stopped watching Neighbours 30 years ago. It was the biggest mistake of my life
  • Stars hit the red carpet for premiere of Game of Thrones prequel House of the Dragon
  • The power of Laurinda: ‘I see myself now. I don’t need you to see me’
  • You think kids can’t process a tale of murder and revenge? Think again
  • ‘Mick Jagger was not gonna marry no black woman’: P.P. Arnold on the ugly face of the swinging ’60s
  • ‘My jaw hit the floor’: Report shows sexual harassment is rife in film and TV
  • Did Malcolm Turnbull’s narcissism destroy the Morrison government?
  • ‘I found my family’: How Michelle Visage became Drag Race’s breakout star
  • ‘Whatever it takes, Bubbi’: Why you shouldn’t refuse TV’s The Offer
  • What does a person have to do to get their name in the dictionary?
  • ‘The shows won’t fit on the stage’: Why Melbourne could see a drought of opera and ballet
  • ‘A real winner’: Paper Girls will fill the Stranger Things-size hole in your life
  • Shopify culls Australian staff in late night raid
  • Majority of animals caught in shark nets last year were threatened or protected species
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden and China’s Xi to speak during tense Taiwan standoff
  • Five dead, dozens injured as 7.0-magnitude earthquake hits Philippines
  • Africa is being pushed to take sides in the Ukraine war
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: U.S. made ‘substantial proposal’ for release of Griner and Whelan
  • Accused Honduran drug cartel matriarch appears in U.S. court
  • U.S. offers deal to Russia to free Brittney Griner as she testifies in Moscow
  • Who is Viktor Bout, Russian arms dealer eyed in rumored prisoner swap?
  • Violence against women rampant under Taliban, new report finds
  • Hungary’s Viktor Orban faces outrage after saying Europeans shouldn’t become ‘mixed race’
  • Emu and chef help stop driver fleeing crash site
  • Pentagon calls out China’s military threats as Taiwan tensions worsen
  • A Congo teen alleged rape by a priest. She had to flee. He can still say Mass.
  • Residential schools banned native languages. The Cree want theirs back.
  • France’s mustard shortage fuels drama and panic in grocery stores
  • How deforestation is pushing the Amazon toward a tipping point
  • Australian rugby league players boycott game over Pride-themed jerseys
  • Filipino lawmaker wants to punish people for ghosting
  • As they wait for weapons, Ukrainians hold the line with Soviet artillery
  • Taiwan hones invasion response amid China’s threats over Pelosi trip
  • Canada says pope's apology to Indigenous not enough
  • Wildfires in Germany, Czechia threatening tourist region
  • Lightning kills nearly 50 this week in India's Uttar Pradesh
  • 'New Cold War': Russia and West vie for influence in Africa
  • Oil block auction in DRC punctures Africa's climate goals
  • Highland Park shooting suspect indicted on more than 100 felony counts
  • Companies made more than $1B selling powerful guns to civilians, report says
  • Uvalde school board calls on Texas to raise minimum age for assault weapons
  • They lowered her casket into the ground. It wasn’t their mother inside.
  • As Mega Millions hits $1 billion, winning doesn’t mean a happy ending
  • Ex-officers sentenced for violating George Floyd’s civil rights
  • A record number of trans and nonbinary people are running for office
  • Man charged with arson at dealership he said sold him busted car in ’86
  • Locals help police capture Oregon arson suspect by tying him to tree
  • A body found in 1948 became an Australian obsession. Now, there’s an ID.
  • Retired barber in Minn. cuts hair for donations — to feed the poor in South Africa
  • 19-year-old turns Gaetz insult into $115K abortion rights fundraiser
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says he’s ‘feeling great’ after testing negative for the coronavirus
  • Co-defendant in Jan. 6 Sicknick assault case pleads guilty
  • The Georgia criminal investigation into Trump and his allies, explained
  • Fed hikes rates by three-quarters of a percentage point to fight inflation
  • Recession experts don’t think we are in one and neither do I
  • Facebook reports first-ever decline in revenue, hinting at darkening economy
  • Federal Trade Commission sues to block Meta’s acquisition of VR company
  • Facebook workers fear cuts after blunt warnings from Zuckerberg, leaders
  • NASA is working to keep space station going despite bluster from Russia
  • Residents find plenty of reasons to stay in Kings Park
  • Natural disasters are intensifying. Is your insurance policy sufficient?
  • Have Putin’s Ukraine Goals Shrunk or Expanded?
  • Tunisia’s Democracy Is Collapsing. Biden Shouldn’t Just Stand By.
  • The Days of ‘Germany Knows Best’ Are Over
  • Why an Up-Your-Nose Covid Spray Will Be Costly
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution: QuickTake
  • Why Poor Nations Now Face a Pandemic Debt Crunch
  • What’s Driving the US-China Spat Over Audits and Delisting
  • Why Blackouts Are Still Crippling South Africa
  • In the rural West, ‘self-reliance’ can take a heavy toll on mental health
  • The Chips Act has only gotten better. Now it’s the House’s turn to act.
  • The chips bill means the Era of Hands-Off Government is over
  • Democrats keep bleeding Latino voters. That’s a huge problem in Nevada.
  • Joe Biden working while covid-sick was not ‘white supremacy’
  • A hero of the Trump right shows his true colors: Whites only
  • BeReal isn’t actually real at all
  • Now we see the wisdom of the high court’s ‘vulgar cheerleader’ ruling
  • The U.S. is a lot stronger than Russia. We should act like it.
  • Is the U.S. economy about to slip on a banana?
  • Pelosi should go to Taiwan — when the time is right
  • When it rains on Donald Trump, it pours. And the deluge is just beginning.
  • The Pulitzers’ Trump trap is every media outlet’s Trump trap
  • Most third parties have failed. Here’s why ours won’t.
  • Here’s what’s offensive about Brad Pitt’s skirt
  • Why I’m protesting the Congressional Baseball Game
  • I don’t want to be connected to my phone at ballgames
  • Remember to take a wider view of Trump’s actions
  • Biden could have set an example
  • We all eat. That’s a lesson.
  • The glass ceiling exists all over in Maryland
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Shell’s profits hit record $11.5bn, as UK households face winter energy bill pain – business live
  • Water is the real thing … but millions of Mexicans are struggling without it
  • Athletic feats at Commonwealth Games cannot distract from Britain’s colonial sins | Tumaini Carayol
  • The 'sadmin' after my mother’s death was hard enough – then I encountered Vodafone | George Monbiot

  • Eritrean refugees say they are being arbitrarily detained in Ethiopian camps

  • Sri Lanka extends state of emergency as police round up protest leaders

  • Premier League 2022-23 preview No 7: Crystal Palace

  • Brittney Griner: US seeking prisoner swap with Russia to release WNBA star

  • Margaret Atwood to publish ‘highly personal’ collection of short stories
  • ‘Imprudent’ priest uses inflatable mattress as altar during mass in sea
  • UK scientists take ‘promising’ step towards single Covid and cold vaccine
  • Shadow ministers question Labour’s stance on strikes after Tarry sacking
  • LIV Golf unveils 14-event schedule with promotion and relegation for 2023
  • Commonwealth Games must confront the truth about its sportswashing past | Andy Bull
  • Diana Kennedy obituary
  • ‘Home is where the heart is’: Leah Williamson insists England are focused
  • David Trimble’s passing shows how much politics has changed – and not just in Northern Ireland | Martin Kettle
  • Marlen Reusser goes it alone to claim stage victory at Tour de France Femmes
  • Germany accuses Russia of ‘power play’ as gas pipeline supply drops by half
  • Clever canines: can dogs really tell when you’re being unkind?
  • Meet the gaymers: why queer representation is exploding in video games
  • Outrage in Brazil as Jair Bolsonaro avoids five charges related to Covid response
  • Does the Covid vaccine really affect your period? Here’s what our study found | Viki Male
  • Premier League 2022-23 preview No 6: Chelsea
  • A day at London Pride – in pictures: ‘We are happy to sweat, feel our legs ache, bolstered by solidarity’
  • TechScape: What should social media giants do to protect children?
  • US sees union boom despite big companies’ aggressive opposition
  • Ukraine steps up attacks on Russian-occupied Kherson
  • Why is everyone saying Instagram is rubbish now – and what’s TikTok got to do with it?
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 28 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-28 07:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Former Hong Kong opposition district councillor jailed for insulting police at protest
  • Hong Kong Observatory marks 22 hot nights in July, most since records began in 1884
  • Hong Kong Financial Secretary Paul Chan says global rate rises, recession will hurt exports, but consumption vouchers will offset impact
  • Hong Kong private hospitals mostly not equipped to treat coronavirus patients, will offer beds to public sector for non-Covid use to ease healthcare crunch
  • More than 100 customers show up at Hong Kong’s Happy Cake Shop to bid farewell to popular bakery ahead of closure
  • Teacher exodus leaves Hong Kong schools scrambling to hire fresh graduates, fill vacancies for senior positions
  • Sell Hong Kong stocks when the likes of HSBC raise prime rates, historical data suggests
  • Hong Kong’s online retail market has become too crowded for e-commerce giant Alibaba, which is closing its local Tmall site
  • Hong Kong raises base rate to 2.75 per cent in lockstep with US Fed’s 75-basis point hike
  • Hong Kong’s brightest should explore mainland science and technology opportunities to reach their full potential
  • Classic Hong Kong bakery Happy Cake Shop to close after 45 years in business as landlord takes back premises
  • UN human rights watchdog urges Hong Kong to take ‘concrete steps’ to repeal national security law; government slams ‘unfair’ criticism
  • Hong Kong power firm CLP to offer HK$100 vouchers to blackout-hit residents, after cable fire causes worst outage in decades
  • Hong Kong butcher may face HK$50,000 fine and jail time after banned chemical found on beef sample
  • Activists plan to vote for ‘Hong Kong parliament’ abroad amid questions about its legitimacy
  • Return of mainland Chinese visitors, launch of ‘insurance connect’ a ‘huge opportunity’ for Hong Kong insurers
  • Hong Kong publisher launches first English volume in chronicle of city’s 7,000-year history
  • ‘Funny prank’ on former Hong Kong leader: ex-aviation worker pleads guilty to criminal damage for changing name of Leung Chun-ying’s son in flight booking and ordering extra services
  • Hong Kong’s anti-graft agency charges 2 men for allegedly urging others to spoil ballots in Legco election
  • Hong Kong police arrest 18 in crackdown on fraud syndicate for sending bogus applications to qualify for jobless subsidy scheme
  • Uygur separatist group rebuilds bases in Afghanistan even as China-Taliban ties grow
  • Joe Biden to bring up Taiwan, ‘excessive’ South China Sea claims in call with Xi Jinping, White House says
  • China, South Korea renew service sector talks, opening up a ‘win-win for both economies’
  • US Senate passes bill providing US$52 billion in subsidies to semiconductor industry
  • China ‘confident’ on 2030 climate goal as summer heatwaves worsen global energy crisis
  • Xi Jinping tells cadres to prepare for ‘critical moment’ for China ahead of Communist Party congress
  • Canadian cancer scientist hoping for role on China’s Tiangong Space Station
  • Huawei launches updated operating system HarmonyOS 3, new ride-hailing platform
  • Call between Xi and Biden won’t break China-US deadlock, observers say
  • China launches new satellite in ‘important step’ towards global quantum communications network
  • US aircraft carrier group heads towards Taiwan as tension over Nancy Pelosi’s possible visit continues to grow
  • Will Chinese President Xi Jinping be given the formal title of ‘the people’s leader’?
  • Chinese EV maker Li Auto’s L9 SUV runs off road, hits barrier during test drive, could derail sales after news goes viral
  • Coronavirus: China’s travel sector sees ‘revenge’ rebound, but outbreaks and lockdowns loom large
  • Chinese, Indonesian first ladies watch a folk music performance in Beijing
  • The US military is making plans in the Indo-Pacific in case Nancy Pelosi travels to Taiwan
  • After decriminalising cannabis, Thailand may gamble on casinos to lure more tourists to revive pandemic-hit economy
  • 1MDB-linked hip-hop artist Pras Michel sells stake in Fugees Library to Apollo-backed firm
  • Will South Korea join US-led chip alliance ‘Chip 4’, and risk business ties with China?
  • North Korea’s Kim Jong-un threatens to use nuclear weapons amid tensions with US, South Korea
  • Fearing US sanctions, Philippines scraps US$227 million deal to buy 16 Russian helicopters
  • Two charged in murder of Ripudaman Singh Malik, Canadian Sikh acquitted in Air India bombing
  • How instability in Afghanistan raises the global risk of narco-terrorism
  • South Korea’s risky ‘Kill Chain’ plans to counter North Korea nuclear threat may raise eyebrows in China
  • Japan’s netizens express glee, relief over execution of Akihabara mass murderer
  • Japan, Indonesia agree to boost naval security ties, with an eye on China
  • Sea mine risk slows rush to extract grain from Ukraine
  • Apple nabs key Lamborghini executive to supercharge its self-driving electric car project after years of setbacks
  • Reduce number of sex partners to avoid monkeypox, WHO urges after declaring global emergency
  • Nasa details plans to bring back Mars rock samples by 2033, two years behind China
  • Joe Biden hails Democrats’ breakthrough on health, climate spending bill
  • In Britain, talk of Boris Johnson’s return to No 10 before he’s even left
  • Russia redeploys troops in Ukraine, switching tactics from offence to defence
  • Former Republicans and Democrats form third US political party, co-chaired by Andrew Yang
  • Blinken says US offered Russia ‘substantial’ deal to free Americans Brittney Griner, Paul Whelan
  • US Federal Reserve unleashes another big rate hike in bid to curb inflation
  • US gun makers made over US$1 billion from AR-15 sales in past decade
  • Joe Biden ends Covid-19 isolation after testing negative twice
  • Critics voice concerns after Scotland puts Ukrainian refugees on cruise ship
  • Iran hanged 251 people at a ‘horrifying pace’ in first half of 2022
  • Scientists warn window to stop monkeypox spread closing fast
  • Rare pink diamond found in Angola may be the largest seen in 300 years
  • Russia claims capture of Ukraine’s Vuhlehirska power station, as UK reports ‘tactical advances’ by Wagner mercenaries
  • Why betting against the Hong Kong dollar peg is a fool’s errand
  • Victor Li’s CK Asset submits bid for China Evergrande’s flagship Hong Kong building in lifeline for distressed developer
  • Alibaba, Meituan are top picks at Daiwa in sector upgrade as internet stocks to benefit most from China stimulus
  • Hong Kong stocks slip with AIA, banks, developers under pressure as rate hike seen weighing on shrinking economy
  • New York-listed Chinese cloud services provider Kingsoft Cloud files to go public in Hong Kong
  • How Southeast Asia can foster an inclusive economic recovery
  • South Korean chip maker SK Hynix reports record revenue despite ‘difficult’ business environment
  • Country Garden, China’s top property developer, plans US$360 million share sale to refinance debt
  • NewJeans, new K-pop group, have YouTube, TikTok abuzz – here’s everything you need to know about them
  • How your liver keeps you healthy and how hepatitis vaccinations and a good diet can protect it
  • Tomb of the River movie review: generic Korean crime thriller starring Yoo Oh-seong, Jang Hyuk mixes bravado and bloodshed
  • A holiday in Saudi Arabia? But what about its human rights record, you ask. If you want squeaky clean, not many countries will make your itinerary
  • How to create the best skincare routine for summer: remember sunscreen on your head, lip care to avoid burns
  • Autistic genius lawyer in Netflix K-drama makes it a global sensation – why Extraordinary Attorney Woo, starring Park Eun-bin, gets everything right
  • Disney is preparing for the metaverse, but what is it, and what can video game platforms Roblox and Fortnite tell us about its potential impact?
  • ‘So much Chinese culture came from the outside’: nomads, the Silk Road and developments in ancient art in northern China
  • Septet: The Story of Hong Kong movie review – Johnnie To-produced feature sees seven directors put their feelings for the city on film
  • What is blokecore? Soccer shirts, Adidas sneakers – think British working men in the 1990s – with fans in Rihanna, A$AP Rocky
  • Why luxury travellers are getting up close with nature – with five-star amenities: Four Seasons Tented Camp, Banyan Tree Group and Bensley Collection offer resort experiences in the wild
  • 6 Korean stars who survived being cancelled by scandals, from AOA’s Jimin and Stray Kids’ Kim Woojin, to Red Velvet’s Irene, Seo Ye-ji and Singles’ Inferno’s Song Ji-a
  • Hong Kong billionaire Pansy Ho at 60: from friendships with Canto-pop icons Anita Mui and Leslie Cheung, to taking over her casino tycoon father Stanley Ho’s business empire
  • STYLE Edit: Richard Mille unveils the world’s thinnest watch – the RM UP-01 Ferrari is a 1.75mm timepiece that perfectly fuses the craftsmanship of luxury watchmaker and carmaker
  • The fabulous life (and wardrobe) of Cardi B’s daughter Kulture: from Dior and Chanel shopping sprees with mum, to Swarovski-encrusted Hermès Birkins, a Rolls-Royce car seat and her own Richard Mille
  • Inside Justin Bieber’s Hawaii holiday rental home, up for auction: the singer paid US$10,000 a night to stay at Waterfalling Estate, which also hosted the Love Island and Ex on the Beach cast
  • Is the Porsche 911 the world’s most timeless sports car? Almost 60 years on, it has the same iconic look while Ferraris and Lamborghinis are continually replaced with newer models
  • Are Zenith watches now as prized as Rolex and Patek Philippe? The LVMH-owned brand’s Chronomaster Sport and Defy Skyline models now have waiting lists as long as the world’s most coveted Swiss timepieces
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • VOX POPULI: In this tiring age of COVID-19, our days of anxiety have returned
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
